purpose: Workstream C fixtures are a two-sided proof: the fixture must be demonstrably RED against pre-fix main, then flipped to enforced-green by the fix bead. This file records the red half so the flip is auditable rather than asserted.

expected_failure.flipped_green_by: A1b (trusted setup first, read-only backstop re-asserted after)
expected_failure.flip_action: delete the #[ignore]; the assertion must not change

red_run.result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 679 filtered out
red_run.panic_message: trusted session setup must run before the read-only transaction opens, or a table-backed application context cannot be established at all (ORA-01456); got backstop at 0 and trusted setup at 2
red_run.root_cause: crates/oraclemcp-core/src/connect.rs profile_session_statements extends with read_only_setup_statements FIRST when level_state.effective_ceiling() == ReadOnly, then login_statements, then login_script, then trusted_session_statements. On a protected profile the backstop opens a read-only transaction before any trusted setup runs.
red_run.consequence: Trusted session setup that writes -- notably table-backed application context, the standard mechanism for driving VPD -- fails with ORA-01456 on exactly the posture the README recommends. The capability is unavailable by construction rather than by policy, and the profile owner has no way to express it.

green_half.result: ok. 25 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored; 0 measured; 654 filtered out
green_half.note: Both postures are pinned so the fix cannot drift sideways. A writable profile must take NO backstop (the fix must not start emitting it where it was never wanted) and a protected profile must KEEP one (the fix must not remove it to satisfy the ordering assertion). The defect is where the backstop sits, not whether it exists.

why_no_existing_test_caught_it.reason: It builds a single profile with no protected flag and no max_level, so the ceiling is not READ_ONLY and the backstop is never emitted at all. The ordering defect is structurally unexercised rather than merely unasserted. Test-shape rule A.8-5: setup ordering is part of the contract and must be asserted per posture.
